idea.hook: Think all black holes are the same size? Prepare for a mind-bending scale comparison!
facts: The smallest known stellar-mass black holes, formed from collapsed stars, have an event horizon only ~30 km across – roughly the size of a city!
Our Milky Way's supermassive black hole, Sagittarius A*, is 4.3 million times the Sun's mass. Its event horizon spans 26 million km – large enough to fit dozens of Suns inside!
But the record-holder, TON 618, is 66 BILLION times the Sun's mass. Its event horizon stretches an unimaginable 390 BILLION km, easily swallowing Pluto's entire orbit over 30 times!
idea.payoff: From city-sized to larger than entire solar systems – the universe's black holes span an incomprehensible range of sizes!
idea.title: Black Hole Sizes: From City to Cosmos! ⚫️🌌

scenes.text: Think all black holes are the same size?
scenes.visual: Abstract cosmic gas and dust swirling slowly, hinting at a gravitational anomaly. No distinct black hole yet.

scenes.text:
Smallest known: ~30km event horizon
Roughly the size of a city!
scenes.visual: A small, glowing black hole appears. A silhouette of a large metropolitan city skyline is shown alongside it for scale comparison.

scenes.text:
Milky Way's Sgr A*: 26 MILLION km
Fits dozens of Suns inside!
scenes.visual: Transition to a much larger, prominent black hole (Sagittarius A*). Multiple sun-like spheres are depicted easily contained within its event horizon.

scenes.text:
Record-holder TON 618: 390 BILLION km
Swallows Pluto's orbit >30 times!
scenes.visual: An unimaginably massive black hole (TON 618) fills the screen. A simplified diagram of our solar system, with Pluto's orbit highlighted, is shown to be tiny and completely dwarfed by the black hole.

scenes.text:
From city-sized to solar system devourers...
Black holes span an INCOMPREHENSIBLE range!
scenes.visual: A rapid visual progression from the smallest black hole to the largest, showcasing the vast scale difference. Ends with an awe-inspiring, dynamic view of a supermassive black hole at the heart of a galaxy, swirling accretion disk prominent.

youtube.title: Black Hole Sizes: From City to Cosmos! ⚫️🌌
